data.title: Favorable Risk-Reward Amid Resilient Demand and Cloud-Driven Upside Despite Pricing and Macro Uncertainties
data.description:
Andrew Sherman, an analyst from TD Cowen, maintained the Buy rating on SAP AG. The associated price target was lowered to $250.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Andrew Sherman has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors including solid underlying demand trends and an attractive valuation. While recent commercial partner surveys showed slightly softer results quarter over quarter, enterprise checks across key industries such as healthcare, manufacturing, retail, and high-tech remained resilient, and partner feedback pointed to robust large-deal pipelines and healthy cloud momentum.

At roughly 16x EV to expected 2026 free cash flow and after a share price pullback of about 25% since the last earnings release, Sherman views the risk-reward as favorable despite macro and pricing-related uncertainties. He acknowledges potential near-term disruption from SAP’s evolving consumption-based pricing and geopolitical risks in energy-related and Middle East markets, but believes expectations have reset lower and sees upside as cloud growth accelerates and customers expand investments in areas such as GenAI and SAP’s newer offerings.

According to TipRanks, Sherman is an analyst with an average return of -11.8% and a 29.00% success rate. Sherman covers the Technology sector, focusing on stocks such as JFrog, Dynatrace, and Datadog.

In another report released on April 13, BMO Capital also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $210.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Microsoft: Long-Term AI and Cloud Tailwinds Justify Higher Target Despite Near-Term Range-Bound Trading
data.description:
TD Cowen analyst Derrick Wood maintained a Buy rating on Microsoft yesterday and set a price target of $540.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Derrick Wood has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Microsoft’s cloud and AI positioning. He expects Azure growth to remain solid, with modest outperformance versus guidance, even as Microsoft diverts more GPU capacity toward first‑party AI applications and internal frontier model development. This disciplined resource allocation may cap near‑term upside but supports long‑term competitive strength.

He also anticipates meaningful momentum in M365 as Copilot adoption scales, supported by a more aggressive paid-seat sales strategy and increased GPU prioritization for Copilot workloads, which should drive incremental revenue acceleration. While rising capex and contained Azure growth could keep the stock trading in a range near term, he views the recent pullback and a valuation around 23x CY26E P/E as attractive, and believes an improving Copilot narrative and higher capex‑driven AI capacity justify a higher price target of $540 and a continued Buy recommendation.

In another report released yesterday, Robert W. Baird also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $500.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
